deliverable.title: Productive Living-World Control Contract
deliverable.summary: A concrete contract that makes intent, execution, verification, challenge, and memory one world-native loop.
system_changes: Hermes Keep commissions a bounded quest record containing raw intent, purpose, assignee, next event, and a proof requirement.
Workhorse Forge may show productive smoke, sparks, and builder motion only while a named driver record is in running state.

payload_markdown:
# Productive Living-World Control Contract

## Decision
The living world is the AI-OS work surface, not a decorative status map. A quest is complete only when it yields an inspectable work product, survives an explicit verification boundary, and is either challenged for refinement or accepted into durable memory.

## System changes
- Hermes Keep commissions a bounded quest record containing raw intent, purpose, assignee, next event, and a proof requirement.
- Workhorse Forge may show productive smoke, sparks, and builder motion only while a named driver record is in running state.
- Proof Arena must distinguish artifact existence from artifact usefulness: it exposes the work product, checks, and unresolved limits before acceptance.
- The owner may challenge a verified relic back to the Forge or accept it for physical archiving in Memory Library.
- Memory Library becomes the durable destination for accepted work; archived relics remain visible world state rather than disappearing into a completion counter.

## Acceptance contract
- Every productive animation is backed by a source path and legal quest state.
- Every completed quest exposes a substantive artifact body, not only IDs, hashes, or PASS language.
- Verification shows what passed and what remains unproved; identity checks never imply semantic quality.
- The owner has an explicit accept-or-challenge decision before durable completion.
- Acceptance changes the world by moving and displaying the relic in Memory Library; challenge visibly returns it to the Forge.

## Critic boundary
- This artifact is deterministic scenario content, not generated by Hermes from arbitrary owner intent.
- The fixture proves interaction and state grammar, not live autonomous task quality.
- Browser-local scenario state is not a curated-vault write and does not establish durable AI-OS memory.
- Owner judgment remains the semantic-quality gate; the fixture checker only verifies source identity, payload hash, and required structure.

## Next executable slice
Wire one local, no-send AI-OS work-order adapter to this exact quest schema. The adapter must write an openable artifact and receipt, then let Proof Arena compare the artifact against task-specific acceptance criteria before the owner can archive it. Keep model calls, spend, external sends, publish, deploy, and curated-vault writes behind their existing gates.
boundary: Scenario artifact only. No model call, workflow mutation, external action, curated-vault write, or live-work claim.
